author: ShawTim
summary: The 30% floor anti-gaming penalty is mathematically dead code. build_investigation_batches creates exactly one batch per dimension, so score_raw_by_dim always has one entry per key. min([x]) == x == weighted_mean, making floor_aware = 0.7*x + 0.3*x = x — an identity function. The anti-gaming claim in the README is unsupported by this mechanism.
caveats: Low practical impact — scores are not incorrect, just not protected by the floor mechanism as advertised
Third iteration by same author on same floor mechanism (S116, S184, S233); S184 verification already documented the identity-function property
Issue pressure and issue cap adjustments still function correctly independent of the floor blend
